text.title: Market Sizing with AI Research Tools Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the market-sizing-with-ai methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Market size MUST be estimated via three methods: top-down (market share), bottom-up (users × ARPU), value-theory (value created × capture rate). Single-method estimates are rejected.

rationale.source: Strategy consulting handbook 2025; AI-market sizing practice 2026

rule.rationale: Each method has bias; triangulation surfaces disagreement and lifts robustness.

rule.statement: Every figure MUST cite (source_url, source_year); figures older than 2 years vs target year MUST be flagged.
rule.rationale: Stale figures are the dominant error mode in AI-mediated market sizing.

rule.statement: If the three methods differ by >2x, the report MUST include a reconciliation section explaining the spread, not pick the favorable number.
rule.rationale: Hiding the spread destroys trust with investors and downstream planners.

rule.statement: AI tool queries MUST set recency_filter='year' (or tighter) for market data; default broader windows include stale figures.
rule.rationale: Perplexity defaults pull from older corpora; without recency filter 2022 numbers leak in.

rule.statement: If stakes < $10k OR niche market without public figures, skip and use 1-1 expert interviews.
rule.rationale: Methodology amortizes only above an effort/stakes ratio threshold.
